feed.title: Mend.io Unified Release Notes
feed.subtitle: Aggregated RSS, Atom, and HTML of all Mend release notes.

entry.title: Mend for Bitbucket DC: Version 25.11.1 (30-November-2025)
entry.content:
Unified Agent 25.11.1-223 | Renovate 41.173.1 | Remediate 25.10.1 | Pre-Scan Builder (PSB) 25.8.1

New Features and Updates

  • The cloneSubmodules feature now supports scanning a repositorys submodules via SSH as well as HTTPS.

  • The following languages and package managers are now officially supported:

    • Node.js 24.x (language)

    • npm 11.x (package manager)

    • Gradle 9.x (package manager)

entry.title: Mend for GitLab: Version 25.11.1 (30-November-2025)
entry.content:
Unified Agent 25.11.1-223 | Renovate 41.173.1 | Remediate 25.10.1 | Pre-Scan Builder (PSB) 25.8.1

New Features and Updates

  • The following languages and package managers are now officially supported:

    • Node.js 24.x (language)

    • npm 11.x (package manager)

    • Gradle 9.x (package manager)

  • SCA Reachability now supports zero day configuration. When enabled, the integration will trigger a reachability scan when a new vulnerability is introduced to a dependency that is included in the users codebase.

Resolved Issues

  • (SAST) Fixed an issue where SAST scans on pull requests targeting an empty main branch produced misleading results and incorrectly reported a successful scan status.

entry.title: Mend for GitHub Enterprise: Version 25.11.1 (30-November-2025)
entry.content:
Unified Agent 25.11.1-223 | Renovate 41.173.1 | Remediate 25.10.1 | Pre-Scan Builder (PSB) 25.8.1

New Features and Updates

  • The cloneSubmodules feature now supports scanning a repositorys submodules via SSH as well as HTTPS.

  • The following languages and package managers are now officially supported:

    • Node.js 24.x (language)

    • npm 11.x (package manager)

    • Gradle 9.x (package manager)

Resolved Issues

  • (SAST) Fixed an issue where SAST scans on pull requests targeting an empty main branch produced misleading results and incorrectly reported a successful scan status.

entry.title: Mend for GitHub.com: Version 25.11.2 (14-December-2025)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • (SCA) An SCA scan will now be triggered when a versions.kt file is added or modified.

entry.title: Mend Developer Platform: Version 25.11.2 (14-December-2025)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • Minor fixes and improvements to the sorting, filtering and loading speed of the repositories and jobs tables in the Developer Platform UI.

entry.title: Mend Unified Agent: Version 25.11.2 (15-December-2025)
entry.content:
Resolved Issues

  • Fixed an issue where invalid system paths in certain PNPM and Yarn projects caused Reachability analysis to fail.

  • Fixed an issue where Go projects with build errors caused all dependencies to appear as direct in the UI, resulting in the loss of transitive dependency hierarchy and breaking vulnerability path analysis. Dependency relationships are now accurately reported even when fallback mechanisms are triggered.

  • Fixed an issue where Erlang dependencies could not be resolved during project scans, resulting in incomplete dependency trees.

  • Fixed an issue where Elixir dependencies could not be resolved during project scans, under certain conditions.

entry.title: Mend CLI: Version 25.11.2.2 (22-December-2025)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • (SCA) Hotfix:

    • The SCA CLI now supports tags with increased key and value lengths up to 255 characters, removes the previous 20-tag limit, and allows a broader range of characters.

    • Added a new CLI parameter, --persist-logs, that allows users to persist all log levels, including INFO, to a specified directory after every SCA scan. This enhancement ensures comprehensive log availability for auditing, compliance, and integration with external log management systems, regardless of scan outcome.

entry.title: Mend AI: Version 25.11.1 (01-December-2025)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • (Premium) Added OAuth support in the Red-Teaming REST API Target integration.

  • (Premium) Traditional Chinese can now be configured as the default language of your application in the Target Configuration for red-teaming.

entry.title: Mend Container: Version 25.11.2 (14-December-2025)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • Mend Container registry integration now supports Google Artifact Registry (GAR).

Resolved Issues

  • Fixed an issue where long package names in the Container package name column were not fully visible, allowing users to view the entire package name without limitation.

  • Fixed an issue where the findings count displayed in the scans section and project overview did not always match the actual number of findings, ensuring accurate and consistent vulnerability counts across all views.

entry.title: Mend SAST: Version 25.11.2 (15-December-2025)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • Improved accuracy of Java, JavaScript/TypeScript and Python analysis, especially for the following CWEs:

    • CWE-78: Command Injection

    • CWE-79: Cross-site Scripting

    • CWE-89: SQL Injection

  • Added support for two compliance standards, PCI DSS 4.0 and MISRA, now visible in the code findings table and code reports.

  • Dead code analysis for Java now excludes generated files and skips scanning those exceeding the maximum file size limit, improving performance and focusing results on relevant code only.

  • Enhanced support for Rust macros, ensuring they are properly parsed to detect tainted data.

  • When reviewing suppression requests, users can now filter by predefined reasonsfalse positive or acceptable riskto make the review process more efficient.

  • Newly created organizations will now have C++ gen 2 engine enabled by default, ensuring all users benefit from the latest version automatically.

  • Scans are now automatically stopped if the CLI cannot retrieve required engine configuration, preventing inconsistent results when some components are unreachable.

  • Added the ability to filter, sort, and group findings by project in the applications view, making it easier to manage and analyze security findings.

Resolved Issues

  • Fixed an issue where the PHP engine could become unresponsive on Windows due to improper timeout handling. The timeout mechanism has been adjusted to restore previous logic on Windows, ensuring stable scanning and preventing unexpected engine hangs.

  • Fixed an issue where importing results from an external SARIF file would fail if the tool name in the imported file contained spaces.

  • Fixed an issue that caused reports to show negative numbers of detected findings.

entry.title: Mend SCA: Version 25.11.2 (14-December-2025)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • Added support for SBOM import through API 3.0, enabling users to upload SBOM files within the application context. A dedicated logs API provides enhanced traceability and integration.

  • Added a new Reports tab in the Accounts view, enabling account administrators to generate and manage Inventory, Security Alerts by Library, and Security Alerts by Vulnerability reports at the account level. Reports use an asynchronous flow with updated email notifications and permissions aligned to the Legacy SCA model.

  • Legal and Compliance workflows now support Jira Issue creation, aligning with other workflows in the Mend AppSec Platform.

    • The Create Issue action for legal findings is available in the OS Inventory table as well as Automation Workflows with Licenses conditions.

entry.title: Mend AppSec Platform: Version 25.11.2 (14-December-2025)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • (Open Beta) A new integration with ServiceNows Vulnerability Response module is now available, enabling Dependencies and Code findings to sync directly from Mend.io into ServiceNow. The integration provides drill-down visibility from Applications to Projects and their findings, along with bi-directional updates.

  • (SCA) Added support for SBOM import through API 3.0, enabling users to upload SBOM files within the application context. A dedicated logs API provides enhanced traceability and integration.

  • (SCA) Added a new Reports tab in the Accounts view, enabling account administrators to generate and manage Inventory, Security Alerts by Library, and Security Alerts by Vulnerability reports at the account level. Reports use an asynchronous flow with updated email notifications and permissions aligned to the Legacy SCA model.

  • (SCA) Legal and Compliance workflows now support Jira Issue creation, aligning with other workflows in the Mend AppSec Platform.

    • The Create Issue action for legal findings is available in the OS Inventory table as well as Automation Workflows with Licenses conditions.
